prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial opens on a very dark, low-contrast frame. Only faint shapes are visible, and the word “McDonald’s” appears dimly near the bottom, but the rest of the scene is unclear.

The next shots show a warm, softly lit restaurant or family setting. A middle-aged man in a dark plaid shirt leans down and smiles toward a child at the right edge of the frame. Small white copyright text appears at the lower left: “©1992 TONKA CORP.”

A following shot shows a smiling blonde woman in close-up, seated at a table. The background is blurred with warm lights and greenery. At the lower left, small text reads “©1992 ORIGINAL APPALACHIAN ARTWORKS.”

The commercial then cuts to close-ups of toys being handled on a tabletop. One shot shows hands holding a brightly colored small doll or figure. White text along the bottom reads: “At Participating McDonald’s® While Supplies Last.” Another close-up shows a small yellow toy dump truck being moved by a hand beside a colorful package or container.

A later shot shows two children at a table, seen in profile and three-quarter view, looking at and handling a small doll figure with yellow hair. The doll is held upright in the foreground. Fine-print caution text appears at the bottom: “Caution: May Contain Small Parts. Ask About Alternate Item For Children Under Three.”

The commercial then switches to a bright green graphic card with festive decorative elements, including holly and starburst shapes. Large stylized text reads: “Mom & Dad Save $50.00.” Small toy-like shapes appear near the lower left.

Another green-framed graphic follows, showing a white panel bordered in red with toy-company logos: “PLAYSKOOL,” “HASBRO,” “MB MILTON BRADLEY,” “PARKER BROTHERS,” and “Kenner.”

The final product display section shows toys arranged on stepped shelves against a light patterned background. First, several Cabbage Patch Kids-style small dolls are displayed with a “Cabbage Patch Kids” logo above them. At the bottom, green text reads “$1.99 Happy Meal,” with the McDonald’s golden arches logo and a small red “McDonald’s” bag icon at the lower right.

The last two frames show a similar display for Tonka toy vehicles. The red “Tonka” logo appears at the top. Five small construction vehicles are arranged on shelves: a blue crane or tow vehicle, a yellow dump truck, an orange cement mixer, an orange fire truck with a white ladder, and a yellow front loader with a black scoop. The bottom again reads “$1.99 Happy Meal,” with the McDonald’s logo and red bag icon at the lower right.
